Let’s see get . First, load package: Now, decide data want generate. (small) example, simulate dataset 250 subjects, belong two cohorts (including 100 150 subjects respectively) underwent two MRI sessions ("01" "02"). interested surface area, generating "area" maps. resolution fake surface maps 163842 vertices (corresponding detailed FreeSurfer template "fsaverage"). make realistic, want inject positive association "age" surface area data (beta = 3.5). association present "frontalpole" region Desikan-Killiany atlas. give everything need play around verywise model fitting. run model using data now, able recover association age located single cluster, perfectly overlapping frontal pole region. neat. hood, simulate_dataset() call two functions: simulate_freesurfer_data() generate brain surface files, simulate_long_pheno_data() generate phenotype data can also call two functions separately, case need one two data sources.
code: library(verywise) #> Loaded verywise 1.3.2.9000 data_structure <- list("cohort1" = list("n_subjects" = 100,                                          "sessions" = c("01","02")),                        "cohort2" = list("n_subjects" = 150,                                          "sessions" = c("01","02")))  # Simulate FreeSurfer and phenotype dataset for both hemispheres for (hemi in c('lh','rh')) {    simulate_longit_dataset(     path = "path/to/verywise/simulated_example",     data_structure = data_structure,     measure = "area",     hemi = hemi,     fs_template = "fsaverage", # FreeSurfer template (largest available: 163842 vertices)     vw_mean = 5, # mean surface area value     vw_sd = 0.1, # standard deviation of surface area values     subj_sd = 0.02, # variability between subjects     site_sd = 0.01, # variability between sites     # (optionally) only simulate data within certain ROIs:     # roi_subset = c('temporalpole', 'frontalpole', 'entorhinal'),      simulate_association = "3.5 * age",     location_association = "frontalpole")  }

title: Optional: simulating data
text: phenotype dataset already, waiting FreeSurfer data cook. case single dataset / cohort called "my_site" 100 subjects underwent three MRI sessions ("baseline", "F1" "F2"). phenotype data ready stored CSV file located "path//my_phenotype_data.rds". dataset contain 300 rows (100 subjects x 3 sessions), variables interest, including "wisdom". Let’s simulate left hemisphere thickness maps, smoothed 10mm FWHM, overall mean 6.5 (mm thickness) standard deviation 1.5. time, save us time, use smaller surface template ("fsaverage3"), 40962 vertices total. also want inject positive association "wisdom" thickness (beta = 10) "entorhinal" "temporalpole" regions.

what: Wait, what is a linear mixed model again?
title: Running vertex-wise linear mixed models
text: linear mixed model (LMM) multilevel regression technique, especially useful observations clustered (e.g. across neuroimaging sites) repeated (across time). nutshell, LMM includes fixed effects (parameters associated entire population) random effects (parameters vary across groups, subjects sites). Later tutorial, point important considerations keep mind building interpreting LMMs. want learn theory behind funky technique, check resources end article.

what: Run a vertex-wise linear mixed model
title: Running vertex-wise linear mixed models
text: main function verywise package run_vw_lmm fits vertex-wise linear mixed model data. Let’s demonstrate simple (probably common) example mixed model, single grouping/cluster (participants) modelled random intercept. function first check inputs run lme4::lmer every vertex left hemisphere. important parameters need know :
code: # Run a linear mixed model run_vw_lmm(   formula = vw_thickness ~ sex * age + site + (1 | id), # model formula   pheno = "path/to/phenotype/data.rds", # or an R object already in memory   subj_dir = "/path/to/freesurfer/subjects", # Neuro-imaging data location   outp_dir = "/path/to/output", # Where you want to store results   hemi = "lh", # (default) or "rh": which hemisphere to run   n_cores = 4  # number of cores for parallel processing )

See next section advice, cheatsheets references model specification. outcome variable one supported brain surface metrics computed FreeSurfer, prefixed vw_: vw_thickness - Cortical thickness vw_area - Cortical surface area (white surface) vw_area.pial - Cortical surface area (pial surface) vw_curv - Mean curvature vw_jacobian_white - Jacobian determinant (white surface) vw_pial - Pial surface coordinates vw_pial_lgi - Local gyrification index (pial surface) vw_sulc - Sulcal depth vw_volume - Gray matter volume vw_w_g.pct - White/gray matter intensity ratio vw_white.H - Mean curvature (white surface) vw_white.K - Gaussian curvature (white surface) currently possible brain metrics outcome variables.

what: Other arguments you may want to specify
title: Running vertex-wise linear mixed models
text: arguments optional, may want specify explicitly depending analysis: outp_dir: character string specifying path output directory, results stored. NULL (default), function creates “verywise_results” sub-directory current working directory (though recommended tidiness reasons). hemi: character string specifying hemisphere analyze. Options: "lh" (left hemisphere: default), "rh" (right hemisphere). folder_id: character string specifying column name pheno contains subject directory names input neuroimaging data (e.g. “sub-001_ses-baseline” “site1/sub-010_ses-F1”). expected nested inside subj_dir. Default: "folder_id". n_cores: integer specifying number CPU cores parallel processing. Default: 1. weights: string numeric vector weights linear mixed model. can use argument specify inverse-probability weights. string, function look column name phenotype data. Note weights normalized standardized way. vector, length number rows data. Default: NULL (weights). seed: integer specifying random seed reproducibility. Default: 3108. save_ss: whether save super-subject matrix (“ss”) .rds file can re-used future analyses. can also character string specifying directory ss saved. TRUE, ss matrix saved <outp_dir>/ss default. Default: FALSE.

previous_headings: Run a vertex-wise linear mixed model
what: Even more arguments for fine-grained control
title: Running vertex-wise linear mixed models
text: advanced arguments typically won’t need, may want tweak depending analytical (control) needs: fs_template: character string specifying FreeSurfer template vertex registration. Options: "fsaverage" (default) = 163842 vertices (–> highest resolution), "fsaverage6" = 40962 vertices, "fsaverage5" = 10242 vertices, "fsaverage4" = 2562 vertices, "fsaverage3" = 642 vertices Note lower resolutions used downsample brain map, faster model tuning. final analyses run using fs_template = "fsaverage" avoid (small) imprecisions vertex registration smoothing. apply_cortical_mask: whether exclude non-cortical vertices analysis. Default: TRUE (recommended). tolerate_surf_not_found: integer indicating many brain surface files listed folder_id column can missing subj_dir. number missing corrupted files > tolerate_surf_not_found execution stop. Default: 20. use_model_template: whether pre-compile model template faster estimation. Default: TRUE (recommended). lmm_control: optional list (correct class, resulting lmerControl()) containing control parameters passed lme4::lmer() (e.g. optimizer choice, convergence criteria, see ?lmerControl documentation details. Default: (uses default settings). chunk_size: integer specifying number vertices processed sequentially chunk (used parallel operations). Larger values use memory may faster depending resources (.e. n_cores). Default: 1000. FS_HOME: character string specifying FreeSurfer home directory. Defaults $FREESURFER_HOME environment variable, set correctly FreeSurfer installation (see Set-article details). fwhm: numeric value specifying full-width half-maximum smoothing kernel. Default: 10. mcz_thr: numeric value Monte Carlo simulation threshold. following accepted (equivalent values separated /): 13 / 1.3 / 0.05, 20 / 2.0 / 0.01, 23 / 2.3 / 0.005, 30 / 3.0 / 0.001, –> default 33 / 3.3 / 0.0005, 40 / 4.0 / 0.0001. cwp_thr: numeric value cluster-wise p-value threshold (top corrections). Set set 0.025 hemispheres analyzed, 0.05 single hemisphere analyses. Default: 0.025. save_optional_cluster_info: whether save additional output form mri_surfcluster FreeSurfer call. See compute_clusters function documentation details. Default: FALSE. save_residuals: Logical indicating whether save residuals.mgh file. Default: FALSE (recommended, files can quite large). verbose: whether display progress messages. Default: TRUE.

Now, let’s organize analyses grid analysis specifications (.e. combinations hemispheres, outcomes, models, datasets etc). row grid correspond single analysis. example want analyze hemispheres (lh rh), three different outcomes (thickness, area w_g.pct), two model specifications: one including interaction sex age (*), one without interaction (+). gives us total 12 analyses (2 hemispheres x 3 outcomes x 2 models). base R function expand.grid() useful way create grid possible parameter combinations, can also create manually prefer (example, certain combinations make sense ). use analysis_id variable select one specific parameter combination (.e. row grid) use define current analysis. example, : . take value hemisphere (just making sure character string factor); b. format outcome variable name correctly (adding vw_ prefix); c. define model formula current analysis (e.g. without interaction term). Finally, let’s run analysis “current” parameters:
code: # Load verywise library(verywise)  # Define project paths and constants ----------------------------------------------  proj_dir <- "path/to/main/project/directory"  fs_home <- "/path/to/FREESURFER_HOME" subj_dir <- "path/to/freesurfer/data"  outp_dir <- file.path(proj_dir, "results")  pheno_filepath <- file.path(proj_dir, 'phenotype_clean.rds')  # this part of the formula will be constant across all analyses covariates <- 'birth_weight + SES + ethnicity'  random_effects <- '(1 | id)'  # Get SLURM parameters ------------------------------------------------------------  analysis_id <- as.integer(Sys.getenv('SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID', unset = 1)) n_cores <- as.integer(Sys.getenv('SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK', unset = 1)) # Define parameter space ==========================================================  hemis <- c('lh','rh') outcs <- c('thickness', 'area', 'w_g.pct') model <- c('*', '+')  param_grid <- expand.grid('hemi'=hemis, 'outc'=outcs, 'mode'=model)  # Select and process current parameter combination --------------------------------  params <- param_grid[analysis_id, ]  hemi <- as.character(params$hemi)  outc <- paste0('vw_', params$outc)  model_spec <- as.formula(paste(outc, '~ age', params$mode, 'sex +', covariates, '+', random_effects)) # Run analysis --------------------------------------------------------------------  output <- run_vw_lmm(formula = model_spec,                      subj_dir = subj_dir,                      outp_dir = outp_dir,                      hemi = hemi,                      pheno = pheno_filepath,                       n_cores = n_cores,                      FS_HOME = fs_home,                      save_ss = TRUE)

title: Run many `verywise` analyses in parallel (on SLURM)
text: fun, still need create SLURM job script (e.g. run_analyses.sh), submit job array scheduler stuff done us. specify many analyses want run (.e. size array), many resources allocate job. , inside project directory, submit job array SLURM : sbatch run_analyses.sh.
code: #!/bin/bash  #SBATCH --job-name=verywise_job_array #SBATCH --array=1-12 # 12 models in total (see above) #SBATCH --ntasks=1 #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=64   # NOTE R limit parallel processes = 124 #SBATCH --time=1-00:00:00    # 1 day time limit, just to be on the safe side #SBATCH --error=analysis_log%a #SBATCH --output=analysis_log%a #SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL #SBATCH --mail-user=your.email@email.com  # Load modules (not always but sometimes necessary on some HPCs) ------------- module load R  # Run analyses (in parallel) ------------------------------------------------- Rscript analysis.R

what: Vertex-wise distributed LMM: per-chunk estimation — chunk_dlmm
title: Vertex-wise distributed LMM: per-chunk estimation — chunk_dlmm
text: Core computational workhorse called run_vw_fed_aggr chunk vertices. Given pre-computed static lambda profile lambda_prof chunk-specific sufficient statistics (\(X^\top Y\), \(\mathbf{1}^\top Y\), \(Y^\top Y\)), function selects optimal \(\lambda\) per vertex returns fixed-effect estimates, standard errors, p-values, variance components.

title: Vertex-wise distributed LMM: per-chunk estimation — chunk_dlmm
code: chunk_dlmm(   lambda_prof,   X1,   XtY,   sumY,   YtY,   n_terms,   chunk_size,   n_sites,   REML,   df )

title: Vertex-wise distributed LMM: per-chunk estimation — chunk_dlmm
text: lambda_prof List. Output static lambda profile loop run_vw_fed_aggr. element named list fields lambda, w (site weights), R (Cholesky factor \(\)), logdetA, logdetV. X1 List length \(K\). element column vector row-sums design matrix site \(k\) (\(X_k^\top \mathbf{1}_{n_k}\)). XtY List length \(K\). element \(p \times C\) matrix \(X_k^\top Y_k\) current chunk \(C\) vertices. sumY List length \(K\). element length-\(C\) vector \(\mathbf{1}^\top Y_k\) (column sums outcome chunk). YtY List length \(K\). element length-\(C\) vector element-wise squared column sums \(Y_k^\top Y_k\). n_terms Integer. Number fixed-effect terms \(p\). chunk_size Integer. Number vertices current chunk \(C\). n_sites Integer. Number sites \(K\). REML Logical. Use REML (TRUE) ML (FALSE) likelihood. df Integer. Residual degrees freedom (\(N - p\) REML, \(N\) ML).

title: Vertex-wise distributed LMM: per-chunk estimation — chunk_dlmm
text: named list four matrices, \(C\) columns: coef \(p \times C\) fixed-effect estimates. se \(p \times C\) standard errors. pval \(p \times C\) two-sided p-values. bw_var \(2 \times C\) variance components; row 1 = \(\hat\sigma^2_\varepsilon\), row 2 = \(\hat\tau^2\).

title: Vertex-wise distributed LMM: per-chunk estimation — chunk_dlmm
text: candidate \(\lambda\) dynamic quantities : $$B(\lambda) = X^\top V^{-1} Y = \sum_k \bigl(X_k^\top Y_k - w_k \mathbf{x}_{k1} \mathbf{1}_k^\top Y_k \bigr)$$ $$C(\lambda) = Y^\top V^{-1} Y = \sum_k \bigl(Y_k^\top Y_k - w_k (\mathbf{1}_k^\top Y_k)^2 \bigr)$$ \(w_k = \lambda / (1 + n_k \lambda)\) site weight. profile (RE)ML log-likelihood evaluated candidate \(\lambda\) maximiser selected per vertex via max.col. Standard errors derived \(\text{Var}(\hat\beta) = \hat\sigma^2 ^{-1}\), \(^{-1}\) obtained via chol2inv pre-computed Cholesky factor.

what: Run federated aggregation for vertex-wise distributed LMM — run_vw_fed_aggr
title: Run federated aggregation for vertex-wise distributed LMM — run_vw_fed_aggr
text: Performs aggregation step privacy-preserving federated linear mixed model (LMM) vertex-wise neuroimaging data. function never sees raw participant data — operates entirely pre-computed sufficient statistics (site payloads) uploaded site. fits random-intercept LMM single shared variance component ratio \(\lambda = \sigma_b^2 / \sigma_\varepsilon^2\), selected via 1-D profile likelihood grid search (rather iterative site communication).

title: Run federated aggregation for vertex-wise distributed LMM — run_vw_fed_aggr
code: run_vw_fed_aggr(   site_names,   formula,   inpt_dir,   outp_dir = NULL,   hemi = c("lh", "rh"),   fs_template = "fsaverage",   fwhm = 10,   seed = 3108,   n_cores = 1,   chunk_size = 1000,   REML = TRUE,   lambda_grid = NULL,   chol_tol = 1e-12,   ridge = 0,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run federated aggregation for vertex-wise distributed LMM — run_vw_fed_aggr
text: site_names Character vector site identifiers. site must corresponding <site>.tar.gz archive inpt_dir. formula two-sided formula specifying fixed effects. LHS names surface measure (e.g. thickness ~ age + sex). inpt_dir Character. Directory containing site payload archives. outp_dir Character NULL. Directory output FBM backing files. Defaults inpt_dir NULL. hemi Character. Hemisphere: "lh" (default) "rh". fs_template Character. FreeSurfer template surface used cortical mask (e.g. "fsaverage", "fsaverage5"). Default: "fsaverage". fwhm Numeric. Full-width half-maximum (mm) smoothing kernel applied site level. Used informational messages ; smoothing must applied payload generation. Default: 10. seed Integer. Random seed passed parallel backend reproducibility. Default: 3108. n_cores Integer. Number parallel workers. 1 (default) runs sequentially; values > 1 use doParallel. chunk_size Integer. Number vertices processed per parallel job. Larger values faster use memory. Default: 1000. REML Logical. TRUE (default), use restricted maximum likelihood (REML); otherwise use ML. lambda_grid Numeric vector candidate \(\lambda\) values grid search, NULL use default log-spaced grid spanning \([10^{-6}, 10^{2}]\). chol_tol Numeric. Minimum diagonal pivot Cholesky factor \(\lambda\) value rejected numerically singular. Default: 1e-12. ridge Numeric. Ridge penalty added diagonal \((\lambda)\) factorisation. Use small positive value (e.g. 1e-6) predictors near-collinear. Default: 0 (regularisation). verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Run federated aggregation for vertex-wise distributed LMM — run_vw_fed_aggr
text: named list four FBM objects, dimension described . Rows correspond model terms; columns vertices. coef \(p \times V\) matrix fixed-effect estimates \(\hat\beta\). se \(p \times V\) matrix standard errors. pval \(p \times V\) matrix two-sided p-values (t-distribution, df degrees freedom). variances \(2 \times V\) matrix; row 1 = \(\hat\sigma^2_\varepsilon\), row 2 = \(\hat\tau^2 = \hat\lambda \hat\sigma^2_\varepsilon\).

title: Run federated aggregation for vertex-wise distributed LMM — run_vw_fed_aggr
text: Model structure. covariance matrix assumed follow $$V = \sigma_\varepsilon^2 (+ \lambda Z Z^\top)$$ \(Z\) block-diagonal indicator site membership. structure, Sherman–Morrison–Woodbury identity reduces \(N \times N\) inverse site-level rank-1 updates, making algorithm \(O(K p^2)\) rather \(O(N^3)\). Grid search. candidate \(\lambda\), function reconstructs global Hessian \((\lambda) = X^\top V^{-1} X\) Cholesky factor, computes profile (RE)ML log-likelihood per vertex chunks. \(\lambda\) maximises log-likelihood selected independently vertex. Chunked processing. Vertices processed chunks size chunk_size keep memory usage bounded regardless surface resolution. Results written directly memory-mapped bigstatsr FBM files disk.

title: Run federated aggregation for vertex-wise distributed LMM — run_vw_fed_aggr
text: Serena Defina, 2026.

title: Run vertex-wise linear mixed model using lme4::lmer() — run_vw_lmm
text: formula model formula object. specify linear mixed model lme4 syntax. outcome variable one supported brain surface metrics (see Details). Example: vw_thickness ~ age * sex + site + (1|participant_id). pheno Either data.frame/tibble containing "phenotype" data (.e., already loaded global environment), string specifying file path phenotype data. Supported file formats: .rds, .csv, .txt, .sav (SPSS). data long format contain variables specified left-hand side formula (.e., ~) plus folder_id column. subj_dir Character string specifying path FreeSurfer data directory. Must follow verywise directory structure (see package vignette details). outp_dir Character string specifying output directory results. NULL (default), creates "verywise_results" sub-directory current working directory (recommended). hemi Character string specifying hemisphere analyze. Options: "lh" (left hemisphere: default), "rh" (right hemisphere). fs_template Character string specifying FreeSurfer template vertex registration. Options: "fsaverage" (default) = 163842 vertices (highest resolution), "fsaverage6" = 40962 vertices, "fsaverage5" = 10242 vertices, "fsaverage4" = 2562 vertices, "fsaverage3" = 642 vertices Note lower resolutions used downsample brain map, faster model tuning. final analyses also run using fs_template = "fsaverage" avoid (small) imprecisions vertex registration smoothing. apply_cortical_mask Logical indicating whether exclude non-cortical vertices analysis. Default: TRUE (recommended). folder_id Character string specifying column name pheno contains subject directory names input neuroimaging data (e.g. "sub-001_ses-baseline" "site1/sub-010_ses-F1"). expected nested inside subj_dir. Default: "folder_id". tolerate_surf_not_found Integer indicating many brain surface files listed folder_id can missing subj_dir. number missing corrupted files  > tolerate_surf_not_found  execution stop. Default: 20L. weights Optional string numeric vector weights linear mixed model. can use argument specify inverse-probability weights. string, function look column name phenotype data. Note normalized standardized way. Default: NULL (weights). lmm_control Optional list (correct class, resulting lmerControl() containing control parameters passed lme4::lmer() (e.g. optimizer choice, convergence criteria, see ?lmerControl documentation details. Default: (uses default settings). REML Logical specifying whether optimize REML criterion (opposed log-likelihood). Default: TRUE. Use REML = FALSE intend model comparison (using AIC output). seed Integer specifying random seed reproducibility Default: 3108. n_cores Integer specifying number CPU cores parallel processing. Default: 1. chunk_size Integer specifying number vertices processed per chunk parallel operations. Larger values use memory may faster. Default: 1000. FS_HOME Character string specifying FreeSurfer home directory. Defaults FREESURFER_HOME environment variable. fwhm Numeric value specifying full-width half-maximum smoothing kernel. Default: 10. mcz_thr Numeric value Monte Carlo simulation threshold. following accepted (equivalent values separated /): 13 / 1.3 / 0.05, 20 / 2.0 / 0.01, 23 / 2.3 / 0.005, 30 / 3.0 / 0.001, \* default 33 / 3.3 / 0.0005, 40 / 4.0 / 0.0001. cwp_thr Numeric value cluster-wise p-value threshold (top corrections). Set set 0.025 hemispheres analyzed, 0.05 single hemisphere analyses. Default: 0.025. save_optional_cluster_info Logical indicating whether save additional output form mri_surfcluster call. See compute_clusters details. Default: FALSE. save_ss Logical indicating whether save super-subject matrix ("ss") .rds file can re-used future analyses. can also character string specifying directory ss saved. TRUE, ss matrix saved <outp_dir>/ss default. Default: FALSE. save_residuals Logical indicating whether save residuals.mgh file. Default: FALSE. verbose Logical indicating whether display progress messages. Default: TRUE.

P-values computed using t--z approximation, cluster-wise correction applied using FreeSurfer's Monte Carlo simulation approach. Multiple Imputation: function automatically detects handles multiple imputed datasets (created mice similar packages), pooling results according Rubin's rules. Parallel processing: verywise package employs carefully designed parallelization strategy maximize computational efficiency avoiding performance penalties associated nested parallelization. Left right cortical hemispheres processed sequentially default. Parallel processing two hemispheres (/different metrics, models) handled user (e.g., using SLURM job arrays similar, see vignette parallelization). Within hemisphere, vertices divided chunks size chunk_size processed parallel across n_cores workers (n_cores > 1). multiple imputed datasets present, processed sequentially within vertex. Note , systems, implicit parallelism low-level matrix algebra libraries (BLAS/LAPACK) can interfere explicit parallelization. feel like processing taking long, recommend disabling implicit threading libraries starting R. example: Also note using large number cores (e.g. >120) may sometimes cause worker initialization issues (e.g. R parallel processes limits) Output Files: Results saved FreeSurfer-compatible .mgh format visualization verywiseWIZard, FreeView neuroimaging software.

what: Simulate a distributed (multi-site) cross-sectional brain surface dataset — simulate_distrib_dataset
title: Simulate a distributed (multi-site) cross-sectional brain surface dataset — simulate_distrib_dataset
text: Generates synthetic distributed dataset site's data lives subdirectory, mirroring real multi-site setup site can access another's raw data. site \(k\) vertex \(v\), generative model : $$   y_{ikv} = \mu_v     + \sum_{j} \beta_j \cdot x_{ijk}     + u_{kv}     + \varepsilon_{ikv} $$ \(\mu_v\) vertex-specific intercept drawn \(\mathcal{N}(\code{vw\_mean},\, \code{vw\_sd}^2)\), \(\beta_j\) fixed effects shared across vertices sites (supplied via betas), \(u_{kv} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \tau^2_v)\) site-level random intercept, \(\varepsilon_{ikv} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2_v)\) residual noise. output folder layout : site folder can therefore passed directly run_vw_fed_local subj_dir matching phenotype.csv pheno.
code: <path>/   <site1>/     phenotype.csv     sub-001/surf/<hemi>.<measure>.<fwhmc>.<fs_template>.mgh     sub-002/surf/...   <site2>/     phenotype.csv     ...

title: Simulate a distributed (multi-site) cross-sectional brain surface dataset — simulate_distrib_dataset
code: simulate_distrib_dataset(   path,   site_sizes = c(site1 = 80L, site2 = 120L, site3 = 60L),   betas = c(sex = -0.2, age = 0.3),   tau2 = 0.01,   sigma2 = 0.01,   fs_template = "fsaverage",   roi_subset = c("temporalpole", "frontalpole", "entorhinal"),   location_association = NULL,   measure = "thickness",   hemi = "lh",   fwhmc = "fwhm10",   vw_mean = 5,   vw_sd = 0.5,   overwrite = TRUE,   seed = 3108,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Simulate a distributed (multi-site) cross-sectional brain surface dataset — simulate_distrib_dataset
text: path Character string. Root directory site sub-folders. Created recursively exist. site_sizes Named integer vector. Names become site/folder names; values give number subjects site. Default: c(site1 = 80, site2 = 120, site3 = 60). betas Named numeric vector fixed-effect coefficients. name must correspond covariate simulated phenotype data. Currently supported covariate names: age Continuous, drawn \(\mathcal{N}(30, 5^2)\) z-scored within site. sex Binary (0/1), drawn \(\text{Bernoulli}(0.5)\). Example: c(age = 0.3, sex = -0.2). Set coefficient 0 include covariate design matrix without injecting signal (useful null-effect benchmarks). tau2 Numeric scalar length-\(V_{\text{roi}}\) vector. -site variance random intercept \(u_{kv}\). value 0 collapses model fixed-effects OLS. Default: 0.5. sigma2 Numeric scalar length-\(V_{\text{roi}}\) vector. Within-site residual variance \(\varepsilon_{ikv}\). Default: 1. tau2 sigma2 together define intra-class correlation \(\text{ICC} = \tau^2 / (\tau^2 + \sigma^2)\). fs_template Character string. FreeSurfer template; determines total number vertices .mgh file. Options: "fsaverage"  = 163842 vertices "fsaverage6" = 40962 vertices "fsaverage5" = 10242 vertices "fsaverage4" = 2562 vertices "fsaverage3" = 642 vertices Default: "fsaverage". roi_subset Character vector ROI names used restrict active vertices (rest set 0 excluded analysis). Vertex locations read FreeSurfer annotation files stored R/sysdata.rda. Default: c("temporalpole", "frontalpole", "entorhinal"). location_association Character vector (optional). supplied, signal encoded betas injected within ROIs; remaining active vertices (roi_subset) generated null model (\(\beta = 0\)). Useful testing spatial localization discovered effects. measure Character string. Surface measure; used file naming . Default: "thickness". hemi Character string. Hemisphere: "lh" "rh". Default: "lh". fwhmc Character string. Smoothing label; used file naming . Default: "fwhm10". vw_mean Numeric scalar. Mean vertex-specific intercept \(\mu_v \sim \mathcal{N}(\code{vw\_mean},\, \code{vw\_sd}^2)\). cortical thickness realistic value around 2.5 mm. Default: 2.5. vw_sd Numeric scalar. Standard deviation vertex-specific intercept distribution. Default: 0.5. overwrite Logical. FALSE phenotype.csv already exists site folder, existing file re-used new brain surface files written site. Default: TRUE. seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. Default: 3108. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Simulate a distributed (multi-site) cross-sectional brain surface dataset — simulate_distrib_dataset
text: Invisibly returns list ground-truth parameters used data generation: beta0 Length-\(V_{\text{roi}}\) vector simulated vertex-specific intercepts \(\mu_v\). betas betas argument supplied. tau2 Length-\(V_{\text{roi}}\) vector -site variances (recycling). sigma2 Length-\(V_{\text{roi}}\) vector residual variances (recycling). u Numeric matrix \(K \times V_{\text{roi}}\) realised site random intercepts. icc Length-\(V_{\text{roi}}\) vector theoretical ICCs: \(\tau^2_v / (\tau^2_v + \sigma^2_v)\). signal_vertices Logical vector length \(n_{\text{verts}}\) indicating global vertex indices received non-zero fixed effect (.e. intersection roi_subset location_association, supplied). Data phenotype files written path side-effect.

Must follow naming pattern "<hemi>.<measure>.<fs_template>.supersubject.rds". folder_ids Character vector folder IDs retain new ss matrix. also column phenotype dataset. error_cutoff Integer indicating maximum number missing IDs allowed function throws error. number missing IDs  <= error_cutoff , warning issued instead. Default: 20. fs_template : (default = "fsaverage") template register vertex-wise data. following values accepted: fsaverage (default) = 163842 vertices (highest resolution), fsaverage6 = 40962 vertices, fsaverage5 = 10242 vertices, fsaverage4 = 2562 vertices, fsaverage3 = 642 vertices Note , moment, used downsample brain map, faster model tuning. final analyses run using fs_template = "fsaverage" avoid (small) imprecisions vertex registration smoothing. new_supsubj_dir Character string indicating path directory new supersubject files stored (either temporarily permanently  save_rds == TRUE . Created exist. n_cores Integer indicating number cores use parallel processing. save_rds Logical. TRUE, new ss also saved .rds file inside new_supsubj_dir. verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

Writes filtered row names ss.rownames.csv new_supsubj_dir.

what: Profile (RE)ML log-likelihood for a single variance ratio — vw_logLL
title: Profile (RE)ML log-likelihood for a single variance ratio — vw_logLL
text: Computes profile (RE)ML log-likelihood (constants) given candidate \(\lambda\) vector residual sum--squares values \(Q\) across vertices. evaluated inside \(\lambda\) grid search select optimal variance ratio per vertex.

title: Profile (RE)ML log-likelihood for a single variance ratio — vw_logLL
code: vw_logLL(REML = TRUE, df, Q, logdetV, logdetA)

title: Profile (RE)ML log-likelihood for a single variance ratio — vw_logLL
text: REML Logical. TRUE (default), evaluate REML objective; otherwise ML objective. df Integer. Degrees freedom: \(N - p\) (REML) \(N\) (ML). Q Numeric vector length \(C\) (chunk size). Residual sum squares \(Q = Y^\top V^{-1} Y - \hat\beta^\top X^\top V^{-1} Y\) per vertex. Vertices Q <= 0 non-finite values pre-set NA caller. logdetV Numeric scalar. \(\log |V(\lambda)|\), computed \(\sum_k \log(1 + n_k \lambda)\). logdetA Numeric scalar. \(\log |(\lambda)|\), computed \(2 \sum_j \log R_{jj}\) Cholesky factor.

title: Profile (RE)ML log-likelihood for a single variance ratio — vw_logLL
text: Numeric vector length \(C\); profile log-likelihood value \(\lambda\) vertex chunk. Vertices NA Q propagate NA.

title: Profile (RE)ML log-likelihood for a single variance ratio — vw_logLL
text: REML profile log-likelihood (constants dropped): $$\ell_{\text{REML}} = -\frac{1}{2} \left[ (N-p) \log\!\frac{Q}{N-p} + \log|V| + \log|| \right]$$ ML profile log-likelihood (constants dropped): $$\ell_{\text{ML}} = -\frac{1}{2} \left[ N \log\!\frac{Q}{N} + \log|V| \right]$$ \(\log|V|\) \(\log||\) pre-computed log-determinants passed logdetV logdetA.

what: Pool lme4::lmer model output across imputed datasets — vw_pool
title: Pool lme4::lmer model output across imputed datasets — vw_pool
text: function combines estimates, standard errors p-values across imputed datasets, single model (.g., one vertex). function largely taken mice::pool() mice::summary.mipo() code. averages estimates complete data model, computes relevant statistics, following Rubin's rules (Rubin, 1987, p. 76). P-values estimation Default: P-values estimated using t--z approach. known anti-conservative small sample sizes provides computationally efficient solution. Type error control addressed rigorously cluster-forming stage. Wald Chi-square test Satterthwaite Approximation residuals model currently simply averaged across imputed datasets, lack better idea combine .

text: out_stats Output single_lmm, .e.: list containing: "stats": dataframe estimates SEs fixed effect term) "resid": vector residuals given model. "warning": character vector warning messages () Note model failed dataset out_stats form list("error"="Error message") m Integer indicating number (imputed) dataset pvalue_method String indicating approximation methods used compute p-values. Options: "t--z", "wald-chi2", "satterthwaite" (see Details). Default: "t--z". min_pvalue Float, used avoid pvalues == 0L log10 Inf. Set 0L trimming applied. Default: 2^-149 ( == 1.401298e-45) smallest positive subnormal float.
